name: Nisshodo Candy Store
tagline: Handmade Japanese mochi and manju since the 1920s — hidden in an industrial warehouse, sold out by noon.
talkStory: The Asato family has been making Japanese mochi and manju by hand in this Kalihi warehouse since 1921, making Nisshodo over a century old. The chichi dango recipe has never changed -- glutinous rice flour, sugar, and potato starch, shaped by hand each morning. During New Year's and Obon season, orders back up for weeks. The industrial setting on Dillingham Blvd gives no hint of the treasure inside. Featured on multiple food shows but stubbornly unchanged by the attention.
